TORNADO THEN STRENGTHENED AND GREW IN SIZE AS IT MOVED EAST WHERE METAL TRUSS TOWERS WERE BENT AND TWISTED TOWARDS THE GROUND ALONG WITH ELECTRICAL TRANSMISSION LINES CONSISTENT WITH EF-3 DAMAGE. MAX PATH WIDTH 350 YARDS. MAX WIND ESTIMATED 140 MPH.

PATH LENGTH 0.15 MILES AND WIDTH 30 YARDS.

AN APPARENT WATERSPOUT CAME ONSHORE NEAR MARKET ST PRODUCING LIGHT WIND TO MODERATE DAMAGE FOR SEVERAL BLOCKS INLAND BEFORE DISSIPATING. DAMAGE INCLUDED LARGE TREE LIMBS,SEVERAL DOWNED TREES, AND DOWNED FENCE. TWO HOMES WERE DAMAGED BY FALLEN TREES. PRELIMINARY RATING OF EF0 TORNADO. DAMAGE PATH 0.25 MILES. MAXIMUM DAMAGE WIDTH 200 YARDS. MAXIMUM ESTIMATED WIND SPEED 85 MPH.
SEVERAL LARGE TREE LIMBS AND TREES BLOWN DOWN... TWO HOMES DAMAGED FROM TREES ON THE ROOF NEAR CANAL ST AND WASHINGTON AVE

LOCAL BROADCAST MEDIA RELAYED REPORT FROM PASCAGOULA POLICE DEPARTMENT OF 12 PEOPLE RESCUED FROM FLOOD WATERS. EIGHT RESIDENTS OF TUPELO AVENUE AND FOUR RESIDENTS OF RAILROAD STREET. EVENT TIME ESTIMATED.
MAIN PASS 289C AWOS STATION KVKY REPORTED A 51 KNOT WIND GUST DURING A THUNDERSTORM.

MOSTLY EF-0 DAMAGE,BUT A LARGE FARMSTEAD HAD TWO BARNS HEAVILY DAMAGED WITH EF-1 TYPE DAMAGE WITH WINDS ESTIMATED AT 100 MPH. LARGE HARDWOOD WERE SPLIT. LARGE ENCASED WALNUTS THROWN AS PROJECTILES THROUGH AIRBORNE SHINGLES. A SMALL LIMB WAS WIND DRIVEN AND WEDGED BETWEEN A WINDOW FRAME AND THE BRICK WALL WITHOUT BREAKING THE GLASS. A SOCIAL MEDIA PHOTO WAS POSTED SHOWING THE TORNADO FROM THE STARTING LOCATION VANTAGE POINT. PATH LENGTH 3.1 MILES. WIDTH 200 YARDS.

MOSTLY MINOR DAMAGE TO HOMES... POSSIBLE TORNADO. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

LARGE WATERSPOUT MOVED ASHORE NEAR SEACLIFF BLVD AND PRODUCED MAINLY TREE DAMAGE TO HARDWOODS AND SOFTWOODS, MINOR AWNING AND SHINGLE DAMAGE, LIGHT OBJECTS TOSSED, TIN SHEETING IN TREES, AND SEVERAL FENCES BLOWN DOWN. DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH EF0, WITH WINDS 85 MPH. PATH LENGTH 2.18 MILES, MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH 250 YARDS WHEN IT CAME ASHORE, REDUCED TO ABOUT 100 YARDS PRIOR TO LIFTING UPON REACHING OLD SPANISH TRAIL NEAR 9TH AVE.

LOCATION WAS ON THORNHILL ROAD NORTH OF THE INTERSECTION OF MANNINGS CROSSING ROAD. NO INJURIES WERE REPORTED. STORM SURVEY INDICATED AN OCCUPIED MOBILE HOME WAS SEVERELY DAMAGED, MOVED OFF ITS LOCATION AND OVERTURNED ALONG THORNHILL ROAD NORTH OF THE INTERSECTION OF MANNINGS CROSSING ROAD. NO INJURIES WERE REPORTED. A LARGE PINE TREE WAS SNAPPED NEAR THE BASE TO THE NORTH OF THE MOBILE HOME. PATH LENGTH ESTIMATED 200 YARDS, WIDTH 50 YARDS BETWEEN MOBILE HOME AND SNAPPED PINE TREE. WINDS ESTIMATED AT 77 MPH BASED ON DAMAGE ASSESSMENT.
